name: sqlite-etl-loader
goal: Build a Python script load_cities.py that: (1) opens a SQLite database at cities.db, (2) creates a table 'cities' with columns id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, country TEXT, population INTEGER, (3) reads the fixture cities.csv (columns: id,name,country,population) and inserts every row into the cities table, (4) runs a query to select the top 5 cities by population and writes them as a JSON array of objects to top5.json. Run the script as a final step so cities.db and top5.json both exist on disk.

notes.purpose: Tier 1 multi-step ETL case. Stresses the model's ability to chain SQLite + CSV + JSON in a single Python script. The min_rows == max_rows == 5 file_shape constraint catches the off-by-one common error of selecting top 4 or top 6.
